- hypothesis and test sheets are prepared faster, with fewer rounds
- quality will be more measurable with at least 3 testable hypothesis indicators
- rework is reduced, because you filter out the mistake of treating assumptions as facts early on

### What happens if you skip it

- the number of misunderstandings increases in the situation of rapid pre-screening of a new service
- it is more difficult to maintain at least 3 testable hypothesis goals

### Realistic expectation
- It will improve: the practical application of idea validation and the quality of the deliverable output.
- Not guaranteed: infallible response without human control in every run.

## How

### Step by step

1. Name the work situation and the expected result exactly. 2. Write down the mandatory limits (time frame, scope, style, prohibitions). 3. Request the first AI version in a specific format. 4. Run a 3-point quality gate, paying particular attention to the risk of **treating assumption as fact**. 5. Fix the output, then save it as a usable template.

### Do / Don't

**Do**
- Enter a short, specific task description so that the format of the hypothesis and test sheet is clear from the start.
- Before finalizing, check whether at least 3 testable hypothesis measurement points have been met.


**Don't**
- Don't leave the topic of treating assumption as fact in the background, even if the answer seems good at first.
- Do not ask for a general text if you need a specific decision for the task of quick pre-screening of material for a new service.

### Common errors and their correction

- **Error:** too general a request for quick pre-screening of a new service. **Correction:** name the person concerned, the deadline and the decision target.
- **Error:** there is no relationship between the output and at least 3 testable hypothesis measurements. **Fix:** record 1 binary pass/fail result at the end of each run.

### It's ready if

- [ ] You have completed and saved **hypothesis and test sheet** output.
- [ ] You checked the output according to at least 3 quality criteria.
- [ ] You have documented the indicator **at least 3 testable hypotheses**.

## Guided exercise

### Inputs

- a short description of a real task about the following situation: **Quick screening of a new service**
- a short own sample that shows what you consider a good idea validation output
- treat the checklist as an assumption as a fact to rule out risk

### Steps

1. Write in 4 lines the situation, the goal and how you will measure at least 3 testable hypothesis results.
2. Prepare a sample request for generating a hypothesis and a test sheet, then request two different versions.
3. Compare the versions in the table and mark which one can go on for real use.

### Expected output format

| Element | What do you type | Expected result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Situation | Quick pre-screening of a new service | Everyone involved understands from one reading |
| Request frame | Target + context + format + constraint | Minimum of 2 usable versions |
| Quality gate | Accuracy + Risk + Next Step | Clear repair decision |
| Final output | hypothesis and test sheet | Saved, reusable template |

> **Pro tip:** hypothesis and test sheet will be stable if you check them with the same 3 questions after each run.

## Independent exercise

### Task

Choose your own task for tomorrow, which requires the same type of decision as in the case of a quick pre-screening of a new service. Apply today's framework so that the end result can be measured in the same way: at least 3 testable hypotheses.

### Output

A short note with three blocks: situation, prompt variants, and quality gate. At the end, write a sentence about what you changed to make your hypothesis and test sheet more accurate.

## Self-check

- [ ] hypothesis and test sheet are ready and can be shared.
- [ ] at least 3 testable hypothesis measurement points are recorded.
- You ran a separate preventive step for the risk of treating assumption as fact.
- [ ] You have a short decision note about why you chose the given version.

### Starting metric

- **Result:** at least 3 testable hypotheses
- **Date:** 2026-02-11
- **Tool used:** ChatGPT or other LLM

## Bibliography

1. **OpenAI Documentation - Guidelines**. OpenAI. 2026. Read: https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides
2. **NIST AI Risk Management Framework**. NIST. 2023. Read: https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework

## Further reading

1. **OpenAI - Request Writing Guide** Why: it gives practical examples with samples that can be adopted immediately. Read: https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/prompt-engineering
2. **Nielsen Norman Group - Surface Copywriting** Why: it helps to confirm the method in real work situations. Read: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/writing-for-ui/

# Lesson 22: Customer profile and value proposition

**Time:** 20-30 minutes

## Learning goal

Formulating the message of a new product. In this situation, you are a product marketer and you have to make a quick but reliable decision.

- repetitive tasks where a clear matching of pain point and offer is important

## When

- you are faced with the task of formulating the message of a new product, and you have little time

**Don't**
- Don't leave too general a message topic in the background, even if the answer seems good at first.
- Do not ask for a general text if you need a specific decision for the task of formulating the message of a new product.

### Common errors and their correction

- **Error:** too general a request for wording the message of a new product. **Correction:** name the person concerned, the deadline and the decision target.

### It's ready if

# Lesson 24: Pricing basics

**In one sentence:** The price is not a guess, but a value positioning.
**Time:** 20-30 minutes
**Tangible output:** 3 option pricing table

## Learning goal

Service introduction with different packages. In this situation, you are a consultant and you have to make a quick but reliable decision.
By the end of today's practical task, you will have a 3-option pricing table.
Key insight: pricing fundamentals start to work when you make the output measurable right from the start.

At the end of the lesson, you will be able to: **match pricing options to target groups**

### Success criteria (observable)
- [ ] The specific daily output is ready: **3-option pricing table**.
- [ ] At least one measurable indicator is attached to the output: **target and justification for all three options**.
- [ ] There is a separate control step for managing the main risk: **underpricing or overpricing**.

### Output you will create today
- **Name:** 3 option pricing table
- **Format:** editable board and short decision note
- **Where saved:** `ai-rutin/nap-24-arazas-alapjai.md`

## Who

**Primary Role:** Consultant
**Secondary Role:** Financial Administrator
**Involved decision maker:** customer

## What

### What is this?

This lesson shows in practice how to apply the **fundamentals of pricing** method in a real work situation.
The goal is not a theory, but an output that you can really use in your next working day.

### What isn't this?

This is not a generic AI demo, and it is not busywork.

### 2-minute theory

- A good price is adjusted to the value perceived by the customer, not just to the cost.
- In real work, a quick fix is ​​more important than a perfect first round.
- Quality comes from ensuring that the 3-option pricing table is verifiable and repeatable.

### Key concepts

- **Working framework:** a short guide that describes the three objectives, format and limits.
- **Quality gate:** quick check before accepting the output.

## Where

### It works fine here

- introducing a service with different packages in situations where a quick and clear decision is required
- when a 3-option pricing table gives several people a clear next step

### Do not use this when

- if you cannot cover the risk of underpricing or overpricing with human control

### Connection points

- daily transfers between consultant and financial administrator
- short statuses and decision preparation notes
- repetitive tasks, where the target audience and justification are important for all three options

## When

### Use it when

- you are faced with the task of introducing a service with different packages, and you have little time
- Based on a 3-option pricing table, several actors must act in the same way

### Frequency

At least once per workday, and every time for recurring tasks.

### Late warning signs

- 3-option pricing table needs to be rewritten every time
- you often get back that the content does not address the problem of under- or over-pricing

## Why it matters

### Practical benefits

- a 3-option pricing table is created faster, with fewer rounds
- the quality will be more measurable for all three options with a target and justification indicator
- rework is reduced, because you filter out underpricing or overpricing errors early

### What happens if you skip it

- the number of misunderstandings increases in the situation of service introduction with different packages
- it is more difficult to maintain a target and justification for all three options

### Realistic expectations

- It will improve: the practical application of pricing fundamentals and the quality of the transferable output.
- Not guaranteed: infallible response without human control in every run.

## How

### Step by step

1. Name the work situation and the expected result exactly. 2. Write down the mandatory limits (time frame, scope, style, prohibitions). 3. Request the first AI version in a specific format. 4. Run a 3-point quality gate, paying particular attention to the risk of **under- or over-pricing**. 5. Fix the output, then save it as a usable template.

### Do / Don't

**Do**
- Enter a short, specific task description so that the format of the 3-option pricing table is clear from the start.
- Before finalizing, check whether the target and justification measurement points for all three options have been met.

**Don't**
- Don't leave the topic of underpricing or overpricing in the background, even if the answer seems good at first.
- Do not ask for a general text if a specific decision requires the introduction of a material service with different packages for a task.

### Common errors and their correction

- **Error:** too general a request for the introduction of a service with different packages. **Correction:** name the person concerned, the deadline and the decision target.
- **Error:** there is no connection between the output and the target receiver and justification measurement for all three options. **Fix:** record 1 binary pass/fail result at the end of each run.

### It's ready if

- [ ] **3 option pricing table** output is ready and saved.
- [ ] You checked the output according to at least 3 quality criteria.
- [ ] You have documented the **target and justification** indicator for all three options.

## Guided exercise

### Inputs

- a short description of a real task about the following situation: **Service introduction with different packages**
- a short sample of your own, which shows what you consider a good pricing base output
- checklist to exclude the risk of underpricing or overpricing

### Steps

1. Write in 4 lines the situation, the goal and how you will measure the target result and justification for all three options.
2. Prepare a sample request for the production of a 3-option pricing table, then request two different versions.
3. Compare the versions in the table and mark which one can go on for real use.

### Expected output format

| Element | What do you type | Expected result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Situation | Service introduction with different packages | Everyone involved understands from one reading |
| Request frame | Target + context + format + constraint | Minimum of 2 usable versions |
| Quality gate | Accuracy + Risk + Next Step | Clear repair decision |
| Final output | 3 option pricing table | Saved, reusable template |

> **Common mistake:** Many people only look at the risk of under- or over-pricing at the end, even though it should already be indicated in the task description.

## Independent exercise

### Task

Choose one of your own tasks for tomorrow where you need to make the same type of decision as in the case of introducing a service with different packages. Apply today's framework so that the end result can be measured in the same way: target audience and justification for all three options.

### Output

A short note with three blocks: situation, prompt variants, and quality gate. At the end, write a sentence about what you changed to make the 3-option pricing table more accurate.

## Self-check

- [ ] 3 option pricing table is ready and can be shared.
- [ ] for all three options, a target segment and justification metric are recorded.
- [ ] You ran a separate preventive step for the risk of underpricing or overpricing.
- [ ] You have a short decision note about why you chose the given version.

### Starting metric

- **Result:** target audience and justification for all three options
- **Date:** 2026-02-11
- **Tool used:** ChatGPT or other LLM

## Bibliography

1. **Atlassian Teamwork Playbook**. Atlassian. 2025. Read: https://www.atlassian.com/team-playbook
2. **OpenAI documentation - Guidelines**. OpenAI. 2026. Read: https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides

## Further reading

1. **Atlassian - Teamwork Playbook** Why: it gives practical examples with samples that can be adopted immediately. Read: https://www.atlassian.com/team-playbook
2. **OpenAI - Request Writing Guide** Why: it helps to confirm the method in real work situations. Read: https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/prompt-engineering

# Lesson 27: 60 second short demo with AI

**In one sentence:** The 60-second short presentation teaches discipline: only the essentials remain.
**Time:** 20-30 minutes
**Tangible output:** 60-second short presentation script

## Learning goal

Presentation to a manager or partner in a short time window. In this situation, you are a business developer and you have to make a quick but reliable decision.
By the end of today's practical task, you will have a short 60-second presentation script.
Key insight: a short presentation structure starts to work when you make the output measurable right from the start.

At the end of the lesson, you will be able to: **create a short and convincing value communication**

### Success criteria (observable)
- [ ] The specific daily output is ready: **60-second short presentation script**.
- [ ] At least one measurable indicator is attached to the output: **short presentation stays between 60-75 seconds**.
- [ ] There is a separate control step for handling the main risk: **collapsing message**.

### Output you will create today
- **Name:** 60-second short presentation script
- **Format:** editable board and short decision note
- **Where saved:** `ai-rutin/nap-27-60-masodperces-rovid-bemutato-ai-val.md`

## Who

**Primary Role:** Business Developer
**Secondary role:** project coordinator
**Affected decision maker:** decision maker

## What

### What is this?

This lesson shows in practice how to apply the **short presentation structure** method in a real work situation.
The goal is not a theory, but an output that you can really use in your next working day.

### What isn't this?

This is not a generic AI demo, and it is not busywork.

### 2-minute theory

- A good short presentation follows a problem-solution-value-CTA logic.
- Quality requires two things: a good task description and consistent control.
- Quality comes from ensuring that a 60-second short presentation script is verifiable and repeatable.

### Key concepts

- **Working framework:** a short guide that describes the three objectives, format and limits.
- **Quality gate:** quick check before accepting the output.

## Where

### It works fine here

- introduction to a manager or partner in a short window of time in situations where a quick and clear decision is required
- when a 60-second short presentation script gives several people a clear next step

### Do not use this when

- if you cannot cover the risk of disintegrating messages with human control

### Connection points

- daily handovers between business developer and project coordinator
- short statuses and decision preparation notes
- repetitive tasks, where a short presentation between 60-75 seconds counts

## When

### Use it when

- introduction to a manager or partner, you are faced with a task in a short time window, and time is short
- Based on a short presentation script of 60 seconds, several actors must act in the same way

### Frequency

At least once per workday, and every time for recurring tasks.

### Late warning signs

- A short 60-second presentation script needs to be rewritten every time
- you often get back that the content does not handle the disintegrating message problem

## Why it matters

### Practical benefits

- a short presentation script of 60 seconds is prepared faster, with fewer rounds
- the quality will be more measurable with a short presentation lasting between 60-75 seconds
- rework is reduced, because you filter out disintegrating message errors early

### What happens if you skip it

- the number of misunderstandings increases when introducing a manager or partner in a short time window situation
- it is more difficult to keep a short presentation between 60-75 seconds

### Realistic expectations

- It will improve: the practical application of a short presentation structure and the quality of the deliverable output.
- Not guaranteed: infallible response without human control in every run.

## How

### Step by step

1. Name the work situation and the expected result exactly. 2. Write down the mandatory limits (time frame, scope, style, prohibitions). 3. Request the first AI version in a specific format. 4. Run a 3-point quality gate, paying special attention to the risk of **message falling apart**. 5. Fix the output, then save it as a usable template.

### Do / Don't

**Do**
- Enter a short, specific task description so that the format of the 60-second short presentation script is clear from the start.
- Before finalizing, check whether a short presentation has been completed, a measurement point remains between 60-75 seconds.


**Don't**
- Don't leave the message topic falling apart in the background, even if the answer seems good at first.
- Don't ask for a general text if you need material for a specific decision, a presentation to a manager or partner in a short time window for a task.

### Common errors and their correction

- **Error:** too general a request for an introduction to a manager or partner in a short time window. **Correction:** name the person concerned, the deadline and the decision target.
- **Error:** there is no connection between the output and the short presentation between 60-75 seconds of measurement. **Fix:** record 1 binary pass/fail result at the end of each run.

### It's ready if

- [ ] **60-second short presentation script** output is ready and saved.
- [ ] You checked the output according to at least 3 quality criteria.
- [ ] You have documented the **short presentation stays between 60-75 seconds** indicator.

## Guided exercise

### Inputs

- a short description of a real task about the following situation: **Introduction to a manager or partner in a short time window**
- a short own sample that shows what you consider a good short presentation structure output
- checklist to exclude the risk of disintegrating messages

### Steps

1. Write in 4 lines the situation, the goal and how you will measure the results in a short presentation of 60-75 seconds.
2. Prepare a sample request for the production of a 60-second short presentation script, then request two different versions.
3. Compare the versions in the table and mark which one can go on for real use.

### Expected output format

| Element | What do you type | Expected result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Situation | Introduction to a manager or partner in a short time window | Everyone involved understands from one reading |
| Request frame | Target + context + format + constraint | Minimum of 2 usable versions |
| Quality gate | Accuracy + Risk + Next Step | Clear repair decision |
| Final output | 60 second short presentation script | Saved, reusable template |

> **Pro tip:** A short 60-second demonstration script will be stable if you check it with the same 3 questions after each run.

## Independent exercise

### Task

Choose a task of your own tomorrow that requires the same type of decision as introducing yourself to a manager or partner in a short time window. Apply today's framework so that the end result can be measured in the same way: a short presentation stays between 60-75 seconds.

### Output

A short note with three blocks: situation, prompt variants, and quality gate. At the end, write a sentence about what you changed to make your 60-second short presentation script more accurate.

## Self-check

- [ ] A 60-second short presentation script is ready and can be shared.
- [ ] short presentation stays between 60-75 seconds, measurement point is fixed.
- [ ] You ran a separate preventive step for the risk of disintegrating messages.
- [ ] You have a short decision note about why you chose the given version.

### Starting metric

- **Result:** short presentation stays between 60-75 seconds
- **Date:** 2026-02-11
- **Tool used:** ChatGPT or other LLM

## Bibliography

1. **Nielsen Norman Group - UX copywriting**. NN/g. 2024. Read: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/writing-for-ui/
2. **Atlassian Teamwork Playbook**. Atlassian. 2025. Read: https://www.atlassian.com/team-playbook

## Further reading

1. **NIST - AI Risk Management Framework** Why: it gives practical examples with samples that can be adopted immediately. Read: https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework
2. **Atlassian - Teamwork Playbook** Why: it helps to confirm the method in real work situations. Read: https://www.atlassian.com/team-playbook

lessons.content:
# Lesson 29: Personal development map

**In one sentence:** Progress is maintained if you have a plan for after the course.
**Time:** 20-30 minutes
**Tangible output:** 12-week development roadmap

## Learning goal

Knowledge retention in a weekly routine alongside work. In this situation, you are a middle manager and you have to make a quick but reliable decision.
By the end of today's practical task, you will have a 12-week development roadmap, which you can use again tomorrow.
Key insight: a 90-day development plan starts to work when you make the output measurable right from the start.

At the end of the lesson, you will be able to: **schedule and measure progress after the course**

### Success criteria (observable)
- [ ] The specific daily output is ready: **12-week development roadmap**.
- [ ] At least one measurable indicator is attached to the output: **12-week checkpoint and metric attached**.
- [ ] There is a separate control step for managing the main risk: **fallback to old operation**.

### Output you will create today
- **Name:** 12-week development roadmap
- **Format:** editable board and short decision note
- **Where saved:** `ai-rutin/nap-29-personal-fejlodesi-terkep.md`

## Who

**Primary role:** middle manager
**Secondary role:** team member
**Involved decision-maker:** direct manager

## What

### What is this?

This lesson shows in practice how to apply the **90-day development plan** method in a real work situation.
The goal is not a theory, but an output that you can really use in your next working day.

### What isn't this?

This is not a generic AI demo, and it is not busywork.

### 2-minute theory

- The plan + measurement together gives momentum. Repetition and reflection preserve knowledge.
- The key is that you always translate the task into a clear output.
- Quality comes from ensuring that the 12-week development roadmap is verifiable and repeatable.

### Key concepts

- **Working framework:** a short guide that describes the three objectives, format and limits.
- **Quality gate:** quick check before accepting the output.

## Where

### It works fine here

- knowledge maintenance in the weekly routine at work in situations where a quick and clear decision is required
- when a 12-week development roadmap gives several people a clear next step

### Do not use this when

- you cannot cover the risk of reverting to the old operation with human control

### Connection points

- daily handovers between middle manager and team member
- short statuses and decision preparation notes
- repetitive tasks, where 12 weekly checkpoints and metrics are recorded

## When

### Use it when

- knowledge maintenance You encounter a task in your weekly routine at work, and there is little time
- Based on a 12-week development roadmap, several actors must act in the same way

### Frequency

At least once per workday, and every time for recurring tasks.

### Late warning signs

- 12-week development roadmap needs to be rewritten every time
- you often get back that the content does not handle the problem of falling back to the old operation

## Why it matters

### Practical benefits

- a 12-week development roadmap is prepared faster, with fewer rounds
- the quality will be more measurable with a 12-week checkpoint and meter fixed with an indicator
- the rework is reduced, because you filter out the error of falling back to the old operation early

### What happens if you skip it

- the number of misunderstandings increases in the situation of knowledge maintenance in the weekly routine alongside work
- it is more difficult to keep a 12-week checkpoint and a fixed goal

### Realistic expectation
- It will improve: the practical application of the 90-day development plan and the quality of the transferable output.
- Not guaranteed: infallible response without human control in every run.

## How

### Step by step

1. Name the work situation and the expected result exactly. 2. Write down the mandatory limits (time frame, scope, style, prohibitions). 3. Request the first AI version in a specific format. 4. Run a 3-point quality gate, paying particular attention to the risk of **falling back to old operation**. 5. Fix the output, then save it as a usable template.

### Do / Don't

**Do**
- Provide a short, specific task description so that the format of your 12-week development roadmap is clear from the start.
- Before finalizing, check whether the 12-week checkpoint and measuring point have been recorded.


**Don't**
- Don't leave the issue of reverting to the old operation in the background, even if the answer seems good at first.
- Don't ask for a general text if you need material for a specific decision, knowledge maintenance in the weekly routine at work.

### Common errors and their correction

- **Error:** too general a request is made for knowledge maintenance in the weekly routine at work. **Correction:** name the person concerned, the deadline and the decision target.
- **Error:** there is no connection between the output and the 12-week checkpoint and meter recorded measurement. **Fix:** record 1 binary pass/fail result at the end of each run.

### It's ready if

- [ ] **12-week development roadmap** output is ready and saved.
- [ ] You checked the output according to at least 3 quality criteria.
- [ ] You have documented the **12-week checkpoint and metric recorded** indicator.

## Guided exercise

### Inputs

- a short description of a real task about the following situation: **Knowledge maintenance in a weekly routine alongside work**
- a short personal sample that shows what you consider a good 90-day development plan output
- checklist to exclude the risk of reverting to the old operation

### Steps

1. Write in 4 lines the situation, the goal and how you will measure the result by recording 12 weekly checkpoints and metrics.
2. Prepare a sample request for the production of a 12-week development roadmap, then request two different versions.
3. Compare the versions in the table and mark which one can go on for real use.

### Expected output format

| Element | What do you type | Expected result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Situation | Knowledge retention in a weekly routine alongside work | Everyone involved understands from one reading |
| Request frame | Target + context + format + constraint | Minimum of 2 usable versions |
| Quality gate | Accuracy + Risk + Next Step | Clear repair decision |
| Final output | 12-week development roadmap | Saved, reusable template |

> **Pro tip:** A 12-week development roadmap will be stable if you check it with the same 3 questions after each run.

## Independent exercise

### Task

Choose your own task for tomorrow, which requires the same type of decision as in the case of knowledge maintenance in the weekly routine at work. Apply today's framework so that the end result can be measured in the same way: 12-week checkpoints and metrics are recorded.

### Output

A short note with three blocks: situation, prompt variants, and quality gate. At the end, write a sentence about what you changed to make your 12-week development roadmap more accurate.

## Self-check

- [ ] 12-week development roadmap is ready and can be shared.
- [ ] 12-week checkpoint and measurement point recorded measurement point is recorded.
- [ ] you ran a separate preventive step for the risk of falling back to the old operation.
- [ ] You have a short decision note about why you chose the given version.

### Starting metric

- **Result:** 12-week checkpoint and measurement recorded
- **Date:** 2026-02-11
- **Tool used:** ChatGPT or other LLM

## Bibliography

1. **OpenAI Documentation - Guidelines**. OpenAI. 2026. Read: https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides
2. **NIST AI Risk Management Framework**. NIST. 2023. Read: https://www.nist.gov/itl/ai-risk-management-framework

## Further reading

1. **OpenAI - Request Writing Guide** Why: it gives practical examples with samples that can be adopted immediately. Read: https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/prompt-engineering
2. **Nielsen Norman Group - Surface Copywriting** Why: it helps to confirm the method in real work situations. Read: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/writing-for-ui/
